skill.name: to-prd
skill.description: Create a product requirements document from the current conversation and codebase context, then publish it to the project issue tracker.
skill.summary: Generate and publish a PRD from existing project context.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static shell-backtick and weak-cryptography alerts are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and ordinary prose. The meaningful risk is that the skill asks the assistant to inspect project context and publish a PRD to an issue tracker, which can expose sensitive planning details if used on private work.

medium_findings.title: Potential Context Disclosure to Issue Tracker
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to use current conversation and codebase context, then publish the PRD to the project issue tracker. This is intended behavior, but it can expose private planning details if the target tracker is public or broadly visible.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly combine current context with issue-tracker publication. The risk depends on tracker visibility, so this is a contextual data exposure concern rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Command Text
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the flagged lines are Markdown inline code. Line 8 names a slash command, and line 20 names a triage label; neither line executes shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are prose in SKILL.md with inline backtick formatting only. No Ruby code, shell invocation, or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography, but the flagged lines contain documentation text. No cryptographic function, algorithm selection, key handling, hashing, or encryption behavior appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are front matter or PRD template prose. There is no code path where a weak cryptographic algorithm could be used.

content.value_statement: Teams lose product detail when planning decisions stay inside chat. This skill turns known context into a PRD and publishes it for triage.
actual_capabilities: Synthesizes a PRD from current conversation context and codebase understanding.
Explores the repository to understand current implementation state.
Uses project glossary terms and relevant architecture decision records.
Structures output with problem, solution, user stories, implementation decisions, testing decisions, scope, and notes.
Publishes the PRD to the project issue tracker with the needs-triage label.
limitations: Requires issue tracker setup and label vocabulary before publishing.
May expose sensitive context if the issue tracker is public or widely shared.
Does not include file paths or code snippets in the PRD.
Depends on existing conversation context instead of a full discovery interview.

use_cases.title: Convert Feature Discussion into a PRD
use_cases.description: Turn an active feature planning conversation into a structured requirements document for team review.

use_cases.title: Prepare Engineering Work for Triage
use_cases.description: Create an issue tracker item that captures implementation modules, test needs, and known scope boundaries.

use_cases.title: Document Codebase-Aware Product Decisions
use_cases.description: Use repository context and project vocabulary to make a PRD fit the existing system design.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic PRD
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after a feature idea has been discussed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PRD from our current conversation and publish it to the issue tracker for triage.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Codebase-Aware PRD
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the implementation must fit existing modules.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the relevant repository context, then create a PRD that uses our project vocabulary and known architecture decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Testing-Focused PRD
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when test planning is important before implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PRD from this context and include clear testing decisions for the modules we discussed.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Triage-Ready PRD
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before sending a complex feature into planning review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Synthesize the full PRD, confirm the major modules and test areas, then publish it with the needs-triage label.

output_examples.input: We discussed adding saved filters to the reporting dashboard.
output_examples.output: A PRD with problem statement, solution, user stories, implementation decisions, testing decisions, out-of-scope items, and further notes.

output_examples.input: The team agreed on a new onboarding checklist flow.
output: A long numbered list of user stories.
Testing guidance focused on external behavior.

output_examples.input: We need a PRD for refactoring billing permissions.
output_examples.output: A codebase-aware requirements document that avoids stale file paths and captures module-level implementation decisions.

best_practices: Run the setup command first so tracker and label details are available.
Review sensitive context before publishing to a shared issue tracker.
Confirm major modules and testing areas when the implementation scope is complex.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when the team still needs discovery interviews.
Do not publish private customer or credential details into public trackers.
Do not treat the PRD as final architecture approval without team review.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a product requirements document from the current conversation and codebase context.

faq.question: Does it publish the PRD?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the assistant to publish the PRD to the project issue tracker.

faq.question: What sections are included?
faq.answer: It includes problem, solution, user stories, implementation decisions, testing decisions, out-of-scope items, and notes.

faq.question: Does it require setup?
faq.answer: Yes. The issue tracker and triage label vocabulary should be available before use.

faq.question: Can it include code snippets?
faq.answer: No. The PRD template explicitly avoids specific file paths and code snippets.

faq.question: Is it safe for private projects?
faq.answer: Use care. It can publish conversation and codebase context to an issue tracker.
